full_context: Calculate the average power consumed by a purely resistive load in a sinusoidal AC circuit.

variable_definitions.name: Average Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Active power consumed by the resistive load

variable_definitions.name: RMS Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: AC voltage RMS value from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given U_rms=220V, R=100Ω, calculate P
task_description: 1. Calculate RMS voltage U_rms; 2. Obtain resistance R; 3. Calculate P = (U_rms^2) / R; 4. Output P.

full_context: Determine the allowable stress based on material yield strength and design safety factor.
potential_questions: Given σ_y=205MPa, n=1.5, calculate [σ]
task_description: 1. Calculate working stress S; 2. Obtain yield strength σ_y and safety factor n; 3. Calculate allowable stress [σ] = σ_y / n; 4. Compare S and [σ]; 5. Output [σ] and judge if strength is satisfied.

full_context: Calculate the output mechanical power of a hydraulic motor, considering volumetric and mechanical efficiency.

variable_definitions.name: Input Hydraulic Power
variable_definitions.meaning: Input hydraulic power from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given P=2000W, η_v=0.92, η_m=0.88, calculate P_out
task_description: 1. Calculate input hydraulic power P; 2. Obtain volumetric efficiency η_v and mechanical efficiency η_m; 3. Calculate total efficiency η_total = η_v × η_m; 4. Calculate P_out = P × η_total; 5. Output P_out.

full_context: Convert the basic rating life of a bearing (in millions of revolutions) to operating life in hours.

variable_definitions.name: Operating Life
variable_definitions.meaning: Expected operating hours at given speed

variable_definitions.name: Basic Rating Life
variable_definitions.meaning: Life in millions of revolutions from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given L_10=125 million revolutions, n=1000 r/min, calculate L_h
task_description: 1. Calculate basic rating life L_10 (million revolutions); 2. Obtain operating speed n (r/min); 3. Convert L_10 to total revolutions: L_10_r = L_10 × 10^6 rev; 4. Calculate L_h = L_10_r / (60 × n); 5. Output L_h.

full_context: Calculate the unbalance caused by a trial mass and its mounting radius on the balancing correction plane.

variable_definitions.name: Trial Mass
variable_definitions.meaning: Test mass used for balancing correction

variable_definitions.name: Correction Radius
variable_definitions.meaning: Distance from trial mass to rotation axis

potential_questions: Given m_balance=5g, r=100mm, calculate U_test
task_description: 1. Calculate allowable unbalance U_per; 2. Obtain trial mass m_balance and correction radius r; 3. Calculate trial unbalance U_test = m_balance × r; 4. Compare U_test and U_per; 5. Output U_test and judgment.

full_context: Verify whether pump installation meets cavitation requirement: available NPSH ≥ required NPSH + safety margin.

variable_definitions.name: Available NPSH
variable_definitions.meaning: Surplus energy at pump inlet from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given NPSH_a=8.2m, NPSH_r=3.0m, S=0.5m, determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Calculate available NPSH NPSH_a; 2. Obtain required NPSH NPSH_r and safety margin S; 3. Calculate NPSH_r + S; 4. Compare NPSH_a and (NPSH_r + S); 5. Output result (Yes/No).

full_context: Judge whether mechanical vibration level exceeds the allowable limit specified by standard.

variable_definitions.name: RMS Vibration Velocity
variable_definitions.meaning: RMS vibration velocity from previous calculation

potential_questions: Given v_rms=7.07mm/s, v_limit=4.5mm/s, determine if exceeded
task_description: 1. Calculate RMS vibration velocity v_rms; 2. Obtain allowable vibration limit v_limit; 3. Compare v_rms and v_limit; 4. Output result (Yes/No).

full_context: Calculate total sound pressure level from two independent sources or a source plus background noise.

variable_definitions.name: Sound Pressure Level 1, 2
variable_definitions.meaning: Two sound levels to be combined

potential_questions: Given L_p=78dB, L_bg=60dB, calculate total level
task_description: 1. Calculate theoretical sound level L_p; 2. Obtain background noise L_bg; 3. Compute 10^(L_p/10) and 10^(L_bg/10); 4. Sum the values; 5. Calculate L_total = 10 × log₁₀(sum); 6. Output L_total.

full_context: Understand the requirements of Safety Integrity Level (SIL) for PFD_avg (low demand mode) and PFH_d (high demand mode).

variable_definitions.name: PFD_avg Range
variable_definitions.meaning: Target failure probability range for low demand mode

variable_definitions.name: PFH_d Range
variable_definitions.meaning: Target failure rate range for high demand mode

potential_questions: Given PFH_d=9e-7 /h, SIL2 range [1e-7, 1e-6] /h, determine if satisfied
task_description: 1. Calculate PFH_d; 2. Obtain PFH_d range for target SIL (e.g., SIL2); 3. Judge if PFH_d is within range; 4. Output result (Yes/No).

full_context: Calculate the maximum allowable stopping time of mechanical moving parts under total safety stop time requirement.

variable_definitions.name: Mechanical Stopping Time
variable_definitions.meaning: Time from brake signal to full stop

variable_definitions.name: Total Safety Stop Time
variable_definitions.meaning: Total safety stop time from previous calculation

variable_definitions.name: System Response Time
variable_definitions.meaning: Delay from trigger to brake actuation

potential_questions: Given t_s=0.3125s, T_2=0.02s, calculate T_1
task_description: 1. Calculate total safety stop time t_s; 2. Obtain system response time T_2; 3. Calculate mechanical stopping time T_1 = t_s - T_2; 4. Output T_1.

full_context: Calculate the maximum bending moment a section can resist.

variable_definitions.name: Bending Capacity
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum bending moment resisted by section

potential_questions: Given W=500mm³, [σ]=180MPa, calculate M_max
task_description: 1. Estimate applied force F; 2. Obtain section modulus W and allowable stress [σ]; 3. Calculate bending capacity M_max = W × [σ]; 4. Obtain applied moment M; 5. Compare M and M_max; 6. Output M_max and judgment.

full_context: Calculate the ultimate tensile strength of a material.

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Load
variable_definitions.meaning: Peak force recorded in tension test

potential_questions: Given F_max=40000N, A_0=100mm², calculate σ_u
task_description: 1. Calculate yield strength σ_y; 2. Obtain maximum load F_max and original area A_0; 3. Calculate tensile strength σ_u = F_max / A_0; 4. Output σ_u.

full_context: Calculate the heating coefficient of performance of a heat pump.

variable_definitions.name: Heating COP
variable_definitions.meaning: Heating output per unit power input

potential_questions: Given Q_h=1200kW, W=200kW, calculate COP_h
task_description: 1. Calculate cooling COP; 2. Obtain heating capacity Q_h and input power W; 3. Calculate heating COP_h = Q_h / W; 4. Output COP_h.

full_context: Estimate total flow coefficient of multiple valves in parallel.
potential_questions: Given C_v=28.3 per valve, two identical in parallel, calculate C_v_total
task_description: 1. Calculate single valve Cv; 2. Obtain number of parallel valves n; 3. Estimate total Cv_total ≈ n × Cv; 4. Output Cv_total.

full_context: Convert mass-based higher heating value to volume-based value.

variable_definitions.name: Volumetric HHV
variable_definitions.meaning: Higher heating value per unit volume

variable_definitions.name: Mass HHV
variable_definitions.meaning: Higher heating value per unit mass

potential_questions: Given HHV=47.226 MJ/kg, ρ=0.8 kg/m³, calculate HHV_vol
task_description: 1. Calculate mass HHV; 2. Obtain fuel density ρ; 3. Calculate volumetric HHV_vol = HHV × ρ; 4. Output HHV_vol.

formula: \frac{p_1 V_1}{Z_1} = \frac{p_2 V_2}{Z_2} (isothermal, constant Z: p_1 V_1 = p_2 V_2)
full_context: Estimate volume after expansion or compression of real gas under isothermal condition with small Z change.

variable_definitions.name: Initial Pressure & Volume
variable_definitions.meaning: Pressure and volume at initial state

potential_questions: Given p1=5MPa, V1=0.449m³, p2=0.1MPa, constant Z, calculate V2
task_description: 1. Calculate initial volume V1; 2. Obtain p1, V1, p2; 3. Use p1V1 ≈ p2V2 → V2 ≈ (p1V1)/p2; 4. Output V2.

formula: Allowable Temperature Rise Limit = Standard Temperature Rise Limit + ΔT
full_context: Under non-standard cooling medium temperature, allowable temperature rise limit is adjusted by ΔT.

variable_definitions.name: Standard Temperature Rise Limit
variable_definitions.meaning: Base allowable rise from design code

potential_questions: Given standard limit=80K, ΔT=32.5K, calculate allowable limit
task_description: 1. Obtain standard temperature rise limit; 2. Obtain adjustment ΔT; 3. Calculate allowable limit = standard + ΔT; 4. Output result.

full_context: Coordinated withstand voltage of equipment shall not be less than representative overvoltage times safety factor to ensure insulation reliability.
potential_questions: Given U_rp=650kV, K_s=1.15, calculate min U_cw
task_description: 1. Obtain representative overvoltage U_rp; 2. Determine safety factor K_s; 3. Calculate U_cw_min = U_rp × K_s; 4. Verify U_cw ≥ U_cw_min; 5. Output verification result.

full_context: Calculate minimum total creepage distance required for insulator based on highest system voltage and unified specific creepage distance.

variable_definitions.name: Unified Specific Creepage Distance
variable_definitions.meaning: Creepage distance per kV of highest voltage

variable_definitions.name: Highest System Voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum line voltage in normal operation

potential_questions: Given USCD=31.5mm/kV, U_m=550kV, calculate L_min
task_description: 1. Obtain corrected USCD; 2. Obtain highest system voltage U_m; 3. Calculate L_min = USCD × U_m; 4. Output L_min.

full_context: ESDD from surface measurement and solution method should be consistent to verify measurement accuracy.

variable_definitions.name: Solution ESDD
variable_definitions.meaning: From cleaning solution salinity and volume

potential_questions: Given Avg ESDD=0.076mg/cm², ESDD_s=0.072mg/cm², calculate relative deviation
task_description: 1. Obtain average ESDD; 2. Obtain ESDD_s; 3. Calculate deviation = (Avg - ESDD_s)/Avg ×100%; 4. Judge if within acceptable range (e.g., <20%).

full_context: Calculate rated current of three-phase transformer from rated power and voltage.
potential_questions: Given S_t=800kVA, U_N=400V, calculate I_N
task_description: 1. Obtain S_t (convert to VA) and U_N (convert to V); 2. Calculate √3; 3. Calculate I_N = S_t / (√3 × U_N); 4. Output I_N.

full_context: Calculate the working safety factor as the ratio of allowable stress to actual stress.

variable_definitions.name: Safety Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of allowable stress to actual stress

potential_questions: Given [σ_H]=600MPa, σ_H=500MPa, calculate S_H
task_description: 1. Obtain allowable stress [σ] and working stress σ; 2. Calculate S = [σ] / σ; 3. Output S.

full_context: At constant power, energy consumption is the product of power and time.
potential_questions: Given P=10kW, t=8h, calculate E
task_description: 1. Obtain power P (kW) and time t (h); 2. Calculate E = P × t; 3. Output E in kW·h.

full_context: Convert bearing rating life from millions of revolutions to operating hours.
potential_questions: Given L₁₀=125 million revolutions, n=3000r/min, calculate L_h
task_description: 1. Obtain L₁₀ and speed n; 2. Calculate total revolutions = L₁₀ × 10⁶; 3. Calculate L_h = (L₁₀ × 10⁶) / (60 × n); 4. Output L_h.

full_context: Calculate heat flow through building envelope under steady-state heat transfer.

variable_definitions.name: Heat Flow Rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Heat per unit time through area A

potential_questions: Given U=0.5W/(m²·K), A=20m², ΔT=25K, calculate Q
task_description: 1. Obtain U, A, ΔT; 2. Calculate Q = U × A × ΔT; 3. Output Q.
